question.text: Within what time period must an intermediary redress investor grievances as per SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008?

answer.text: Not later than thirty days of receipt
Not later than sixty days of receipt
Not later than forty-five days of receipt
Not later than ninety days of receipt

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Section 6.2, the intermediary shall make endeavours to redress investor grievances promptly but not later than forty-five days of receipt thereof. **The Trap:** 30 days is a commonly used timeline in many regulations and may be confused here. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Timely grievance redressal is a key compliance parameter monitored by SEBI during inspections.
